title: Better logging in monday code
excerpt: Monday code’s logging is very limited compared to other off-the-shelf products. It would be great if we could access the logs using something like Datadog, where we could easily search for logs by tags and create dashboa…

posts.author: PluginGenie (Oliver Albrecht)
posts.cookedHtml:
Monday code’s logging is very limited compared to other off-the-shelf products. It would be great if we could access the logs using something like Datadog, where we could easily search for logs by tags and create dashboards for common logging queries. Though whatever logging service that is used would need to have the same level of security as monday code’s environment (SOC 2, HIPAA, etc), otherwise it would defeat the main purpose of using monday code.

I would even be fine paying for it, since I know logging can get expensive depending on the volume.

In addition, segmenting the logs based on the version of the app makes it difficult to find all the logs if an app is routinely updated. It would be great to be able to search for logs across all versions of the app.
